rulings.Case Title: MAJUA & NOYON LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY VS. MG2 LLC, A WYOMING LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEFENDANT RENEE BOISVERT'S PETITION TO VACATE CONTRACTURAL ARBITARATION AWARD
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion calendar for Monday, January 6, 2025, Line 1. 1 - DEFENDANT RENEE BOISVERT'S PETITION TO VACATE CONTRACTURAL ARBITRATION AWARD. Continued to February 3, 2025, for the moving party to provide courtesy copies in compliance with SF Local Rule 2.7B. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/BZ)

rulings.Case Title: MISSION BAY BLOCK 7 HOUSING PARTNERS, L.P. A VS. NIBBI BROS. ASSOCIATES, INC., A CALIFORNIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Good Faith Settlement Betweeen Plaintiff Mission Bay Block 7 Housing And Cross- Defendant Tommy Siu Dba Tommy Siu And Associates Is In Good Faith In Accordance With Section 877.6 Of California Code Of Civil Procedure
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion calendar for Monday, January 6, 2025, Line 10. CROSS DEFENDANT TOMMY SIU DBA TOMMY SIU & ASSOCIATES' Motion For Good Faith Settlement Betweeen Plaintiff Mission Bay Block 7 Housing And Cross- Defendant Tommy Siu Dba Tommy Siu And Associates Is In Good Faith In Accordance With Section 877.6 Of California Code Of Civil Procedure. Evidentiary Objections to the Burtt declaration are overruled. Objections go to the weight of the evidence, not its admissibility. Motion is granted. The Court has carefully reviewed the expert declarations submitted by the parties. Although the Court has overruled the objections to Burtt declaration the Court gave it little weight when compared to the background and stated experience of Sui's experts - Tofani and Conner. Both these experts attribute the project's failures primarily to the work done by Nibbi with little or no liability placed on Sui. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/BZ)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Vacate Dismissal Under C.C.P. 664.6 & Enter Judgment Pursuant To Stipulation
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion calendar for Monday, January 6, 2025, Line 2. PLAINTIFF WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A.'s Motion To Vacate Dismissal Under C.C.P. 664.6 & Enter Judgment Pursuant To Stipulation. Although timely filed the motion is unopposed. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/BZ)
